steps.title: Use the scale factor
steps.content: Each side of triangle U equals $k$ times the corresponding side of triangle R. Since U's side length is greater than R's side length, the scale factor must satisfy $k>1$.

steps.title: Determine what scale factor k must satisfy
steps.content: Side of U = k × side of R. For U's side to be greater than R's side, we need k > 1.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice numerically
steps.content:
$\frac{31}{30}$ $\frac{31}{30} \approx 1.033 \gt 1$ — U's sides are longer than R's ✓
B) $1$ — sides are equal, not greater
C) $\frac{30}{31} \approx 0.968 \lt 1$ — U's sides would be shorter
D) $0$ — sides collapse to length zero

steps.title: Select the valid value
steps.content: Only $\frac{31}{30}$ exceeds 1. That matches the correct answer - A is correct.
